textPlain: Saw this too, but seems a little too good to be true tbh. I don't doubt it helped them, but it's just so hard to make these agents work reliably. And the more "rules" one accumulates, the less reliably they follow all of the rules and details. Heck, Claude can't even follow a single rule of not inserting comments everywhere.. Plus, code often ends up in a mess, without strict human oversight.Perhaps I'm overlooking something though, it would be great to see more concrete details of their implementation, rather than this high-level inspirational description..

I don't believe it.  If i don't explicitly tell claude to admonish itself in CLAUDE.md, it will quickly revert to making mistakes it was making an hour ago before compaction.sometimes it even misses things in CLAUDE.md
Ah yes "compounding engineering", another buzz word.
